cards.title: Cashless Claims
cards.content:
ΓÇó Available only at Network Hospitals.
ΓÇó TPA (Third Party Administrator) issues a Pre-Authorization letter.
ΓÇó Insurer pays the hospital directly.

cards.title: Reimbursement Claims
cards.content:
ΓÇó Treatment taken at Non-Network Hospitals.
ΓÇó Insured pays the hospital bill from their pocket first.
ΓÇó Later, submits original bills and Discharge Summary to the insurer for refund.

items.text: Third Party Administrator: An IRDAI licensed entity that processes claims and manages hospital networks on behalf of the insurer.
Providers of healthcare. They provide a Discharge Summary which is the most critical document for claims.
The policyholder who must intimate the insurer within 24-48 hours of emergency admission.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported): Claims that have occurred but not yet notified. Insurers must maintain reserves for these.
Discharge Summary: A detailed clinical report of the patient's stay, treatment, and condition upon leaving the hospital.
Fraud: Deliberate acts like faking documents, inflating bills, or hiding a Pre-existing Disease to get a claim.
Personal Accident Claims: Pays 100% of Sum Insured for Death/Permanent Total Disability, and a percentage for Partial Disability.
A health insurance claim is triggered by "Hospitalization" (usually minimum 24 hours), except for specified Day Care procedures.
